title: plectranthus reticulatus
intro: Plectranthus reticulatus, also known as the 'Swedish ivy' or 'Cape periwinkle', is a popular, flowering, evergreen perennial herb native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming the leaves of Plectranthus reticulatus has been associated with various health benefits, such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and aiding in digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or capsules for its medicinal uses, and its leaves can also be used topically in the form of a poultice or salve.
